task_completion.description: Navigate to the SEB Server login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open displaying the SEB Server login screen at http://localhost:8080.
The SEB Server dashboard or main administrative interface is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the user management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser shows a user management page or a list of users and their assigned roles within the SEB Server interface.

task_completion.description: Create the admin_audit.json file in the Documents directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a file named 'admin_audit.json' being created or edited in the '~/Documents' (or '/home/ga/Documents') directory.

task_completion.description: Format the JSON file with the required keys
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the JSON file show a valid JSON array of objects. Each object contains exactly three keys: 'username', 'full_name' (combining first name and surname), and 'role'.

task_completion.description: Include only administrative users in the JSON file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file contains entries exclusively for users with administrative roles. No non-administrative users are included in the array.

integrity.description: Agent used the SEB Server web interface to find users
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the SEB Server GUI in a browser, rather than using terminal commands to dump the database directly.

integrity.description: Agent extracted data from the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must be seen viewing the user list or user details in the SEB Server web interface before or while writing the JSON file, proving the data was not hallucinated or copied from an external source.

integrity.description: Agent saved the JSON file to disk
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor should show the file as saved (e.g., no unsaved changes indicator), or a terminal command like 'cat' or 'ls' should confirm the file exists at the correct path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is exactly /home/ga/Documents/admin_audit.json.
